element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player establishes identity before competition; name, appearance, handedness selection
baseline stats visible; player learns variation exists between fighters

element_introduction_order.element: training activities (weights, diet, karate, health club)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player learns to improve attributes incrementally; some training methods are unconventional (iron gum shield, karate)
player progresses linearly up ranking; Evander Holyfield is final boss at rank 1
player discovers consistent punch damage; learns localized damage mechanics for head and body
player learns knockdown threshold and win condition
punishing concentrated attacks to weakened areas triggers early fight end
player learns scoring only reflects knockdowns or prevents draws; judges ignore domination without knockdowns
visual feedback for sustained head punishment
optional psychological gameplay (boxers taunt each other)
player discovers career arc is finite; attributes weaken after ~20-25 fights
new high-ranked unseen opponents appear; difficulty spike after title acquisition
hidden Easter egg; requires player to discover entry condition

typical_relief_triggers: between-round timer (opportunity to rest and train)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: round-based tension cycles with inter-round recovery; career mode provides meta-level pacing through 40-fight lifespan; difficulty escalates gradually through rankings then spikes at challenger matches

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: The Beast unlockable boxer (name entry mechanic)

difficulty_curve.early_game: low-rank opponents (28-20) serve as baseline; player learns damage and knockdown mechanics; attributes easily trainable
difficulty_curve.mid_game: mid-rank opponents (19-2) gradually increase fighter quality; player must manage attribute degradation; training becomes essential for progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Evander Holyfield (rank 1) features maximum stats on all attributes; challenger matches introduce previously unseen opponents with high skill; career retirement at 40 fights creates urgency
notable_spikes: first encounter with Holyfield (rank 1 champion with perfect stats)
post-title challenger phase (introduces new high-tier fighters)
late career (attribute degradation makes winning increasingly difficult after ~25 fights)

notable_level_patterns.description: player must defeat 28 ranked boxers in sequence to claim heavyweight title; ranking system creates linear progression with increasing opponent quality

player cannot brute-force through opponents; must invest in training between fights to improve power, speed, stamina, and defense; unconventional training methods (karate, health club visits) add flavor
40-fight limit creates biological decay mechanic; player's boxer becomes weaker after ~25 fights; forces strategic decision-making about when to pursue title vs. preserve competitive window
after defeating Holyfield, new high-ranked opponents appear; creates secondary difficulty spike and extends replayability; previous champion (Holyfield) degrades if defeated
